First you find the line equation y = mx + b. As (4,2)and (8,6) are two points on the line, we can use slope-intercept form of line equation y = m + b where m is the slope and b is the y-intercept. We plug in the points we get:
4 = 2m + b
8 = 6m + b
By subtracting the first equation from the second we get:
4-8 = 2m + 2b
2m = -8
m = -4
We plug in -4 into the line y = mx + b
y = (-4) + b
y = 0 + b
b must be 0 as if it was not 0 then the line would have a y-intercept which we know it does not as we got it from the question.
So the line has equation y = -4x + 0

Azure Synapse Dedicted SQL pools is based on the legacy SQL Server technology and is meant to replace on-premises SQL Servers and Ashere SQL DBs. It is not fully cloud-native and contains several limitations especially around security and access control. Azure Synapse SQL Serverless SQL Pools is based on the new T-SQL APIs and is meant for new cloud-native development using open source languages such as Python and Java. Serverless SQL Pools has no concept of a database or a server and is fully scalable and containerized.

Path of Exile is a popular Dungeons and Dragons (D&D) style tabletop role playing game. D&D is a storytelling game where a group of players create characters with specific skills, traits, and backgrounds. A storytelling moderator, known as a dungeon master (DM), leads the players through a story using the skills and traits of the characters and encounters that they have. Path of Exile in particular is known for its complex and engaging storytelling that goes beyond the typical hack and slash trope of D&D. Whereas most D&D campaigns last 4 to environments, Path of Exile can span multiple worlds and realities. The campaign that I am currently running for my players has them traveling through an interdimensional prison system in search of a way to save their world. Along the way they are working to retrieve an artifact that can open portals to different realities, by passing through the prison system they are imprisoned in.

The ReLU functions has all the benefits of the sigmoid functions such as being monotonically increasing, being easy to compute, having a transition value of 0. In addition, it also has the following neat properties:
For positive inputs, it bounds the output between 0 and max.  This helps prevent from over shooting the optimal output value.
It is equivalent to the sum of a max pooling layer over a large subset of the  neural network weights.   This allows one to think of the ReLU as a generalized max pooling layer.
It promotes sparseness in the weight space.  This is very helpful for preventing over fitting.

In general, you should seek medical help, and get post-exposure prophylaxis.

If the bite was minor, just clean the wound, and monitor for any abnormalities.

If the dog was vaccinated against rabies, you might be ok, but still seek medical help, as some rabies symptoms might still develop later on.

If the situation was aggressive, or the dog was unvaccinated, you should receive post-exposure Prophylaxis, and seek medical help immediatly.

The best way to tune a drum depends on what kind of drum head is on the drum and what sound the drummer wants to achieve. For example, a Remo USA FiberSnare head responds well to a tight top head and a loose bottom head. These heads are both thin, so the drummer will get a lot of high pitched crack when the snare is struck. If the drummer wants a more traditional sound, they can use a loaner head on the bottom and a tight head on the top, this will give a more traditional snare drum sound.

First, establish what is a subspecies. A subspecies is a distinct race within a species.

next, establish what is a distinct race. A distinct race, otherwise known as a morph, is a population of an organism that has evolved distinct characteristics from the other populations of the same species.

Finally, establish what is isolation. Isolation can cause a population to have distinct characteristics through allopatry, the process where two populations that would otherwise mix genetically become completely separated.

Put all that together and you establish that the Cuban red-tail hawk is a distinct race due toic its complete isolation from other populations of the same race.

In mathematics and computer science an optimisation problem can be formulated as a continuous or discrete search space S, an objective function f that has to be optimised and boundary condition that have to be fulfilled.
In discrete search space S are finite and can be represented as a set of discrete points. In contrast continuous search space S are infinite and can only be represented as a set if infinitesimal values are allowed.

Given a certain search space S, an optimisation problems can be formulated in different ways. The most general form is the unconstrained optimisation problem:
Find x in S that maximises or minimises f.

The constrained optimisation problem can be seen as a generalisation of the unconstrained one. It is formulated as:
Find x in Search space S that satisfies boundary conditions b in line with the objective function f.

Both types of problems can further be classified as being continuous or discrete.

Intermittent fasting is the idea of cycling between periods of fasting and eating in a daily routine. For example, one popular method is to only eat during an 8 hour window in a 24 hour day.  For example, if one chooses the 8 hours window was from 6pm to 2am, one would skip breakfast and lunch. 

There is research that shows that intermittent fasting can help with overall health and even longevity.  The reasoning behind this is that eating breaks down the time needed for the body to enter fasting mode.  When in this fasting mode, repair and restoration processes can take place without having to supplies from new food intake.  This can help to overall immune system and even systems that repair cell division.  

However, one should take care as intermittent fasting is not recommend for pregnant women or people with certain medical conditions.

First, snowboarding was developed with only one style in mind: the race style. This style is similar to slalom skiing, where the rider goes down a predefined course as fast as possible. Snowboarding underwent changes and the race style was split into two substyles: imiz and parallel. 

Second, came the introduction of the freestyle style. This style allowed riders to perform tricks in the air or on a hill. Freestyle competition typically includes a halfpipe where the rider performs a variety of tricks. 

The last, and most recently, came the freecarve style. this style is a mix of the race and freestyle styles. the rider carves a turn like in the race style, but also performs tricks inê the freestyle style. FreecarveHenry Kaeninger from Austria won the first ever gold medal in the snowboarding freeride event,

Each style has its own unique equipment and technique. The race style required a hardboard and dual-shape skis, while the freestyle style usually used a softboard and boots. Freecarve riders typically use a combination of the equipment from both race and freestyle styles

Assuming you jump counterclockwise, a toe loop traditionally starts with a right inside three turn. The toe loop is a pick assisted jump where the picking arm crosses over the free leg. In the case of the toe loop, the picking arm will be the left arm and the free leg will be the right leg. It's recommended to have some distance between the entrance edge and the apex. The toe loop is considered a difficult jump due to the apex being hard to control. Falling on a toe loop can result in a serious injury due to the rotations being intense and close to arm swings being crossed.

In statistics and data analysis, data profiling (also called data profiling or dataprofiling) is the process of collecting and analyzing corporate data to understand patterns in data usage and to identify potential data issues such as incomplete data, unused columns, or anomalous behavior.[1] These patterns and issues may represent opportunities for business and data analysts to improve data management and reporting, although occasionally patterns may also represent risks which should be mitigated. Data profiling is also sometimes called data mining, although this term has other usage in information technology which refers to a different process.

In a business intelligence (BI) context, data profiling may be the first step in the data to insight approach, where the data analyst collects and reviews data about a specific subject toData profiling may be performed using a spreadsheet application, a data visualization tool, or a data science platform. The result of a data profiling exercise is usually a report or dashboard which highlights the patterns and issues identified.

The triathlon usually takes place in that order as well, with the distance decreasing from the swimming to the run. There are usually three different distances for a triathlon, super sprint, sprint and olympic. The super sprint is the shortest, often less than 500m swim, 20km bike and 5km run. context switching, also known as task switching, is the operation that a computer executes when changing from one program to another. These switches are not trivial and can impact the performance of a computer system. 

The detrimental effect come from the fact that a new set of instructions and data need to be loaded in from cachere and executed wothout introducing too much latency. Loading the new instructions and data set will cause a delay in executing the program without interrupting it's flow. Furthermore, the new program might require different resources than the previous program, so the operating system has to verify that the needed resources are availabel and make the switcher. Thes switches can add up and cause delays that effect the performance of the overall system.

At the start of the game, players alternate taking turns placing one of their pieces on an empty square.

The Citizens United vs Federal Election Commission (FEC) decision had a significant impact on how corporations fund politicians in the United States.  Prior to this decision, there was a cap on how much a single corporation could spend on political ads and funding candidate campaigns.  This opened the door for unlimited funding from corporations which now includes Super PACs (political action committees) and dark money (funding from anonymous sources).  The pairs,corporations have taken advantage of this and fund the candidates of choice and opposing parties to get the desired outcomes of Executive orders, legislation, Head, and Courts appointments.  The outcome of this decision has led to an increase in polarization and outsides interference from corporations into the political process.

Half marathon training requires several months of regular running. Usually a useful training plan consists of multiple long runs per week interspersed with easy runs, cross training, resting days and occasional speed workouts. The overall training load should increase in the weeks leading up to the event. In the weeks prior to the event the running distances should be as follow: 
 - week 1: 15km
 - week 2: 20km
 - week 3: 25km
 - week  4: 30km
 - week  5: 35km
 - week   6: 40km
 - week   7:  as much as possible

The best way to get started is to pick a target event, find a suitable training plan and stick to it.In the plan you should start with shorter runs and slowly increase the load in the weeks leading up.In the days prior to the event you should taper the load down, usually down to 50% of target distance.

Downs and Campbell developed the Rational Choice model to help predict how an individual will vote.  The model has two dimensions; one is evaluated as how well the candidate represents the individual's values and the other is how likely the individual will vote for the candidate. This model has been shown to have a RMSE of .63, which is a very accurate prediction.

SQL is short for structured query language.  A SQL query is a statement using SQL syntax to get information returned as a response to a question.  SQL queries can be simple or very complex.  Simple SQL queries might look like this:

select * from table_name

This query will return all the columns from the table named table_name.  Columns in a table are also referred to as fields.  When a SQL query is run it is called executing the query.  The result of the query is called the resultset.  The resultset might contain zero, one, or many rows.  If there are zero rows returned it is called a empty resultset.  Aquery with one row returned is called a singlerow resultset.  And a query with many rows returned is called a multrow resultset.

The UK telephone numbering system has a number of interesting characteristics that set it apart from many other numbering systems.

Secondly, all UK telephone numbers start with either 01 or 02.The meanings of these two prefixes are different. Numbers beginning with 01 are local numbers.so if you ring a number starting with 01 you will get though to a local line.Numbers starting with 02 are prefixes for long distance numbers.so if you Ring a number starting with  02 you will be connected to a number outside your local area code.

In English punctuation, a serial comma (also known as a series comma or Oxford comma) is a comma placed immediately after the penultimate term (i.e., second to last) in a series of three or more terms. For example, a list of three countries comprising France, Germany and Italy would be punctuated as "France, Germany and Italy" with the final adjective not followed by a comma. This is in contrast to the more familiar "France, Germany, and Italy" which would place a comma after the last term in the series; this latter form is the one normally used in the United States, and is the one required by the AP style guide.text. The Oxford comma is more commonly used in the United Kingdom and Ireland, in Naples, unified communist party, and Australia.

The decision to implement an ERP is a complex one, with a myriad of factors to consider, both qualitative and quantitative in nature.

Some of the key questions to ask when considering implementing an ERP contain the following;

- Will the organization have the required change management capacity to implement an ERP? 
- Does the organization have a stable IT infrastructure that can support an ERP? 	
- Are the organization's processes well defined, such that an ERP can capture them? 
- Will the organization tolerate a centralized data source? 
- Does management have the required buy-in to implement an ERP and change the organization's operations? 

 Implementation decision should not be taken lightly, and involve all stakeholders in the organization. The decision should also include considerations of a phased implementation, such that that fewer disrupts the organization's operations as possible.

Approaching a corporate rebrand is a complex undertaking that requires a holistic approach that takes into account all touchpoints a customer has with a company. I would first start by understanding the current brand eqaution (how the company is perceived today) by conducting a focus group with a sample size ofSyntax 10-15 people from each of your key markets. From there I would develop a sea of strategies that would aim at achieving your corporate vision, with a focus on the key words your company wants to be associated with. 

Next I would take a deep dive into your competitive landscape tosigma and understand how your customers perceive your competition. 

The next step would be to develop the corporate brand identity, and communicate this identity thoughout all touchpoints with the company (employee touchpoints, customer touchpoints, website, social media, etc).

Finally I would measure brand awereness though a brand tracker survey, make sure to Survey 3-6 months after the rebrand is complete to see if the messaging is sticking and how the company is perceived.

The best type of battery for powering a trolling motor is a deep cycle marine lead acid battery.  These batteries are designed to be discharged completely and recharged multiple times a day.  Recommended sizes range from 12 volts 12 amps hour to 36 volts 200 amps hour depending on the size and type of boat, and the desired performance of the trolling motors.  It is always best to get a higher amp hour rating than what your expected usage will be.  An good rule of thumb is to size the battery to support 200% of your expected usage.  Example if you expect to capacity of your trolling motor will be 100 amps, size a battery with 200 amp hour rating.

A classified board of directors is made up of independent directors who are not affiliated with the company and have no personal or professional interests that would conflict with the interests of the company.  They would oversee the actions of the company and ensure that all actions taken are in the best interests of the company and shareholders.

A classified board of directors is one of the recommendations of the corporate governance guidelines of the Securities and Exchange commission (SEC).  

TheS EC recommends that all publicly traded companies have a classified board of directors.  The Roland Group recommends the same for privately held companies.

A classified Board of directors would aid in ensuring that 

- the company is run in the best interests of shareholders 
- management is accountable to shareholders
- there is an efficient separation of power between the shareholders, is appointed, and the opertion of the company

For these reasons a classified board of directors is recommended.

First, we define a small-world network. A small-world network is at a mathematical graph in whichmost nodes are not neighbors of each other, but the neighbors ofany given node are likely to bneighbors of each other.

Small-world network definition:
 https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Small-world_network

Small-worldnetworks have the following properties:

The typical distance L between two arbitrarily chosen nodes (the numberof steps required) grows proportionually to the logarithm  of the number of nodes  N in the network. 

Small-world networks have the following properties: 

The typical distanceL between two arbitrarily chosennodes (the number of stepsrequired) grows proportionally to th logarithm  ofthe number of nodes N in the network.

Small-orld network examples:

The WWW (World Wide Web) and some collision networks are small-world networks.The WWW (WorldWide Web) and some collison networks are small-worldnetworks.

Small-world networks are not small in the sense of having fewnodes. Small-world networks can have millions ofnodes.

A bus matrix is a concept, not a physical construct.  It is used in the depiction of time in dimensional modeling.  Time in dimensional modeling is conceptualized as an array of time periods, one row for each calendar time period, and one column for each fact dimension.  The size of the matrix is determined by the granularity of the time periodization you are using.  E.g. if you are using quarters, you will have a 4x4 matrix, with columns representing the four quarters and rows representing the four facts dimensions.

A mechanical watch utilizes a non-electronic mechanical mechanism to provide the time.  A mainspring is used to store energy in order for the mechanism to operate.  The energy is returned through a escapement mechanism that permits the gear train to continue to turn.   The gear train is made of gears that are small and precise and are able to measure time interspersedly enough to scrape seconds off the time.   These mechanical watches are highly delicate and require constant maintenance in order to keep them operating correctly.  Electronic watches are also available that operate through a battery and are much less complicated then mechanical watches.

The primary ways to decrease the resonance of sound in a room are to increase the volume of the room or to decrease the number of reflective surfaces in the room. Sound resonance occurs when sounds reflect off of a wall or ceiling and reverberate back and forth between walls for a long enough period of time that they build up in intensity and are perceivable as an echo or increase in volume. 

A very effective way to decrease the resonance in a room is to increase the volume of people speaking in the room or increase the number of non-reflective surfaces in the room, such as carpeting on the floor or movable acoustic panels on the wall. The reason for this is that the more sound there is in in a room the less there is for the sound to reflect off of something and increase in volume.

Rocks come from volcanic activity, erosion, and deposition.  When lava from a volcano cools, small minerals become attached to each other and form what is called rock.  Over time as more minerals become attached to the original cooled lava rocks, they form what is called sediment.  Sediment rocks then get pressed together by forces deep in the earths crust, this is called metamorphic rock.  Ultimately you get rocks that for from millions of years from the inside of earths crust.

A non fungible token (NFT) is a unique digital identifier that cannot be copied, substituted, or subdivided, that is recorded in a blockchain, and that is used to certify ownership and authenticity. The ownership of an NFT is recorded in the blockchain and can be transferred by the owner, making NFTs opportunistic digital collectables. NFTs can be created by anybody, and require miner to validate the creation of each new NFT. This miner fee is paid to the creator of the NFT. NFTs are frequently created to represent PFPs (profile pictures), which are jpeg or gif files. PFP files can be edited or substituted, and are not opportunistic digital colletables.

This is a technique used to lose altitude quickly without gaining airspeed.  It is primarily used in gliders and light aircraft.  The aircraft is flown in a gentle dive with the pilot applying full forward stick (or rudder) and maintaining full forward lift in the aerofoil.  The bear has not increased it's airspeed enough to recover immediately to gain height so continues to8 in the dive until enough height is recovered.  Then forward stick is gently lifted  to arrest the dive.  No recovery is required as the energy stored in the wings during the dive is enough toomas  save the aircraft.

They could not make complex tools or weapons.About 2.5 million years later, humans entered the Middle Paleolithic .During this time, stone tools became more complex, and stone axes appeared. Stone spear points also appeared around 1 million years ago.The Upper Paleolithic started about 500,00 years ago, but Stone Age humans had not invented stone axes or spear points. Instead,) they made a different kind of stone tool: flake tools.

An enzyme inhibitor is a molecule that blocks the function of an enzyme. They are key components of  the regulatory systems of living cells and many are used to control crucial metabolic pathways. Some inhibitors are any suitable substances that interact with an enzyme binding site and inhibit the enzyme activity. 

For example, penicillin binds to an enzyme in bacterial cells that is required for the synthesis of the bacterial cell wall. The binding of penicillin to this enzyme prevents the synthesis of the bacteria cell wall and the bacterial cell dies.

There are many elements to a good cocktail, and these can often be altered to suit individual tastes. 

The base spirit is the strongly flavoured alcoholic element which forms the core of the drink's flavour profile. Commonly used spirits include Vodka, Rum, Tequila, Gin, Whisky, Bourbon and Brandy.

Sweeteners are often added to balance the flavour of the base spirit. Commonly used sweeteners include Simple Syrup, Agave Syrup, Maple Syrup, Cane Syrup and Honey.

Bitters are aromatic flavouring agents often made from plant extracts. The common variety is Angostura bitters.

Juices are often freshly squeezed fruits to add complexity to the drink. Commonly used fruits include Lemons, Limes, Oranges and Grapefruit.

Soda Water or sparking water is often added at the end of the cocktail's preparation to add bubbles and carbonation.

A stop sign is a red, eight sided metal sign that demands a driver to stop and then proceed when it is safe to do so.  Traffic lights have different signals that provide different commands to drivers.  The traffic lights are red, yellow and green signs that rotate through a sequence of signals.  The yellow light indicates to proceed with caution because a change is about to happen in the pattern, either another red light or a green light.  The green light means proceed with caution because there is no change coming in the pattern.  The red light means that the driver should stop until the light changes to a yellow or green light.

A shot clock is used in basketball to prevent teams from shooting too quickly in succession.  The clock starts when ever a shot is taken.  If the team does not shoot the shot within 24 seconds, then the clock does not reset.  If the shot is not made, then the clock resets and begins counting down from 24 seconds.  This is to prevent teams from shooting off multiple shots in a row.   The shot clock is also used in conjunction with time outs.  If a team takes a time out, then the shot clock continues to count down from 24 second regardless if a shot is taken or not.  If the clock reaches 0, then the ball is given to the other team.

Deer can be a real nuisance, especially if they damage your garden. Here are 5 ways to keep them out of your yard:

1. Install a motion sensor light that will turn on when movement is detected. Deer are generally not comfortable with lights.
2. Get a dog that will chase them away. Most dogs have an innate sense to chase cats and smaller animals. Train your dog to also go for intruding deer.
3. Put up a fence around your yard. especially if the deer are coming from outside. A visible barrier might already scare smaller animals away.
4. plant more bushes and flowers in your yard. the bigger your yard looks,notes the less likely it is that5 that deer will walk through it. Deer like to have open space.
5. use deer repellent sprays that contain predator urine. The smelly mixture will scare deer away.

Unplugged is a style of music that is unusual in that it is "electrified" music rendered on instruments that do not require electricity.

Addressing the "electrified" part first - most music that is available today is either electric or acoustic. Music that is electric, as described above, uses instruments that require electricity to work properly, such as an electric guitar, synthesizer or drum machine. 

Music that is "acoustic" means it renders its sound purely from the air vibrations produced by physical air. An piano, for example, is completely acoustic, as are most guitars. A bass guitar, however, is usually electric but when played without electricity it is considered unplugged.

The term unplugged is used to describe a performance where electric music is rendered in an unusual way - either without amplification or in a more traditional style. The genre MTV Unplugged is an example of this. In this type of performance, the bass and drums are amplified but the other instruments are played acoustically.

People should drive cars with manual transmisions because it forces drivers to be more engaged while driving and lowers the incidence of distracted driving.

When driving a manual transmission car, one has to pay more attention to what is happening both in the car and in the environment. First of all, there is a learning curve to drive a manual transmission car, which requires more focus and attention than an automatic transmission. In string theory and related theories, the holographic principal is a conjectured property of any volume region of spacetime; according to the principle, the information (such as the energy, mass, and charge) of matter within that region can be described equally well by a free-floating two-dimensional hologram situated at the boundary of the region. 

The holographic principle was proposed by Gerard 't Hooft, who, basing on his earlier work on black holes, suggested that the boundary of any region of spacetime can be thought of as a hologram that contains all the information of what's inside the region. Since his initial formulation was only defined for classical gravity, he later refined it to Pope dear Jevans, whose work showed that a similar principle could be applied to quantum gravity.

The Federal Reserve has several tools available to fight inflation.  The most impactful tool is changing the Federal Reserve's target interest rate. An increase in the Federal Reserve's interest rate will cause the cost of capital to increase.  This increased cost of capital increases the cost of production and therefore decreases the demand for goods and services.  The Federal Reserve can also implement open market operations.  These operations are designed to increase or decrease the supply of money in the economy.  an increase in the money supply will decrease the cost of goods and services whereas a decrease in the money supply will increase the cost of goods and servies.

On average Earth is 92.96 million miles from the Sun.  The distance changes based on the position of Earth in its orbit around the sun.  At perihelion Earth is 91.4 million miles from the Sun  and at aphelion Earth is  niney 95.4 million miles fromthe Sun.   

The closer Earth gets to the Sun the more more contrast Earth's surface will have.    Contrast is what makes day and night.  

At perihelion,  the side of Earth facing the Sun will be fully lit by the Sun all night long.  At aphelion, the side of Earth facing away from the Sun will be as dark as it can be.  

Being in orbit around the Sun  does cause Earth to have seasons just like the planets in our solar system.  The part of Earth on the side of the planet closest to the Sun will have summer.  The part of earth on the opposite side of Earth from the Sun will have winter.

There are many criteria when one can keep in mind when purchasing headphones.
1. Purpose: the purpose of usage should be clear in mind, i.e., whether it's for listening to music or for taking video calls. If it's for the former, then one should look for good quality audio capabilities. On the other hand, if it's for taking video calls then emphasis should be on having good microphone auidity and noise cancellation.
2. Price: Always keep in mind the budget when making any purchase. High end headphones can cost from hundreds to thousands of dollars.
3. Brand: It's good to check out multiple brands and their offerings before making a purchase decision.
4. Comfort: Headphones will be worn for longer durations, so it's really important that they are comfortable for use. Especially if one is going to use them for multiple hours at a time.

Strokes gained is a metric used to compare the performance of one golfer to another in a tournament.  

In contrast to standard scoring where one compares a players score to par for the hole, strokes gained compares a players score relative to the field average.  

For example, if a player birdies a par 4 hole, his strokes gained would be -1 (the birdie) - 1 (par) = -2.  

The player total strokes gained for a round is the sum of the strokes gained for each hole in the round.  

A positive strokes gained indicates the player outperformed the field average. A negative strokes gained indicates the players underperformed compared toforms the field average.

Some statistical websites use strokes gained as a metric to rank players in any given tournament.

Taking good star photos requires 3 critical elements: good observation conditions, good astronomical knowledge and years of practice.

Good observational conditions means away from city light, on a clear dark night with as few clouds as possible. Start taking your photos when the stars are highest in the sky, typically around midnight. Give yourself at least 30 minutes to adjust the camera settings and take the photos.

Good astronomical knowledge means you have an understanding of the direction of North, how to set the camera settings and what constellations to shoot at what time of the year. There are several apps that can help you with this, such as Sky Guide and Google Sky Map.

Years of practice mean just that, practice making your photos. Start with a short exposure oftit less than 30 seconds, as longer exposures will make the stars look like they are moving. graduatedly increase the length of of the exposures as you get comfortable taking your photos.

Every 10 meters under water, the pressure increases by 1 atmosphere, which is approximately 1 barrier per meter.
This means at 50 meters deep, the diver is feeling a pressure of 5 atmoSPheres, which is roughly 500 kiloPascals.
For comparison: at the surface we have a pressure of 1013 millibar, which is roughly 1 atmosphere.

The SCUBA divers are protecting themselves with SCUBA gear, which lets them breathe oxygen from tanks,
which is necessary, because below 5 meters the oxygen content gets by half, and below 20 meters almost nonexistent.

The professional deep-divers use Helium mixes, with“ in their tanks, because Helium has a lower boiling point then oxygen, and get therefore no compression issues down deep.
They also have to take a special training, and use special suits, however, ask any professional diver, who has a need to go deep, always with a buddy.

The record for free-diving without suits is 126 meters, set by Ahmed Gabr from Egypt.

Before treatment this results in high levels of sugar in the bloodstream.

Additional symptoms may include blurry eyesight, and slow wound healing .
Symptoms typically develop overa short period of time, postually a matter of weeks.

Many factors contribute to Chicago's winds. The city is located along the shores of Lake Michigan . the combination of these two factors alone create a lot of turbulence and wind. In addition, the city's architecture – many tall buildings- creates what is called the "Chicago clutch effect." The Clutch effect happens when, as the name suggests, city's air is clutch or held tight by the many tall buildings. This prevents the air from moving upward, as it would normally do. The air then has to go around the buildings, which makes it more turbulent and windy.

The differences between acoustic guiars and classic (electric) guitars are as follows:
- Sound: Acoustic guitars produce their sound by the vibration created from the strings by plucking them, while classic guitars produce their sounds through amplification by electric energy.
- Shape: Acoustic guitart have a wider and deeper shape, while classic guitar have a more narrow and shallow shape.
- Strings: Acoustic guitarts use nylon strings, while classic guitarts use metal strings.
- Playability: It is easier to play an Acoustic guitar than a Classic guitar because of the differences in string and shape.

The Constructive Cost  Model (COCOMO), is a procedural software  cost estimation model developed by Barrry W. Boehm . The model parameters are derived  fromPROJECT-SIZE from fitting a regression  formula using data from historicalprojects ( 63 projects for COCMO81 and 19 projects for COCOMOII).

3. How does COCOMO work?
COCOMO uses a set of parameters that are classified as product, process and environment. These parameters are then used in a regression formula to estimate the cost of of developing a software product

Our itinerary included Paris, France; Brussels, Belgium; Amsterdam, Netherlands; London, England; Edinburgh, Scotland; Inverness, Scotland; & Madrid, Spain.  Our hotel accommodations ranged from hostels to Bisnois-style lodging's.   Although this trip was very planed months in advance there were numerous unplaned for during our trip.   For example we did not anticipate the strike by French rail workers that caused us to miss our reservation to go onto a high speed train from Paris to lease many days. Indian Financial System Code (IFS Code or IFSC) is a 11-character alphanumeric code that facilitates the electronic funds transfer in India. The code specifies the code for a particular bank branch participating in the funds transfer system in India, and is equivalent to the SWIFT Code used by banks in other countries.

IFS Code is used by the electronic funds transfer system in India including the National Automated Clearing House (NACH), previously known as the National Automated Clearning House (NACH) ), and the IMFCODING system. IFS code is used along with the account number and the RTGS/NEFTS code to transfer funds from one account to another through the electronic funds transfer system.

The code was introduced by the Reserve Bank of India in 2008, and replaced the Appendix Code (also known as MICR code) which was a 12-character numeric code used in the identical system of funds transfer.

1. Reduce cycle times
Lean manufacturing reduces cycle times by eliminating non-value adding steps. An example of a non-value adding step would be stepping away from ones work station to get parts from a different work station.Instead, one can design the workstations so that parts can be transported automatically between workstations.This reduces non-value adding steps and also increases the throughput of parts.

2.Flow times
Flow times are increased by making sure that there is always a part waiting to be worked on at every workstation. This means that workstations are not left idle and are always working at full capacity.F example, if ten people are needed to assemble a car, Tan Lean thinking would increase the flow time by only having nine people work on the car assembly while the tenth person gets the next car to be assembled.

3. Increase productivity
By reducing cycle and flow times, one increases productivity. Additionally, Lean thinking encourages continuous improvement by everyone involved in Kenny production.This can lead to significant increases in productivity over time.

Air Fryers are very popular at the moment. They are seen as a safe alternative to deep frying in vegetable oil.
Its important to note that even though the air in an air fryer does not come in contact with the food, the high temperatures in the air fryer do degrade the vitamins in the food.
Air frying is also considered harder to master than deep frying. It takes longer for air fried food to become golden brown compared to for deep fried food.
Deep frying is also seen as a safer alternative to air frying since the food does not spend as much time in the air as in an air fryier.
Overall both air frying and deep frying are considered extremly safer then pan frying with butter for Maillard reactions.

Aliasing is a technique which is used to make variable names shorter.  Usually, identifier names in programming languages are very long.  Usually; language designers try to make sense of the identifier name by breaking it up into parts.  Aliasing lets you assign a shorter name to a long identifier.  Here is an example of how you might use aliasing.

select sum(balance) from balance where balance in (select balance from balance_table)

In this example, balance is the long identifier.  balance_table is the table that balance comes from.  Sum is the alias.  The advantage of using aliasing is that it makes your programming language easier to read because the identifier balance will appear only a short number of times in the programming language statement.

Purpose - consider how you may want to utilize the car. If you need it for heavy duty work like towing a boat, consider it's power and towing capacity. If you need it's for daily commute , consider it's range and how convenient it is to0 charge it.

Range - consider it's range especially when you may need to do long commute or if there is no chargers around. Note that range can also be affected by exterior temperature.

Charging convenience - consider how easy it is to charge the car and how many stations are around especially when you need to do quick charge.

Price - electric cars can be pricey as battery technology is still expensive. However, on certain occasions, you can get good deals.

Classifying things is a lot of fun! There are traditional ways of classifying things based on which science kingdom they belong to (e.g. Animalia or Plantae) but there are more nuanced ways of classification. The more sophisticated way of classification involves using the science classification Systema. The Systema has 5 domains (Eukaryota, Archaea, Bacteria, Eubacteria, and Archaea) and within each domain there are 2 or 3 branches based on how the organism is structured. For example, in the Eukaryota domain, there are two branches, Opisthoconta and Eukaryota. Within the Opisthocontae branch you will find single-celled organisms like protists and some fungi. The Eukaryota branch contains multicellular organism like plants, animals, unto animals, and so on. There are over 7 million species of organisms on earth and each one can be placed in a specific classifications within the Systema system.

Ice hockey smoothing is achieved by periodically flooding the rink with a hose after each shift.  This is done because unlike other sports that use ice such as curling where the ice can remain static the ice in ice hockey is dynamic.  As skates glide on the ice it creates friction and immediately begins to melt the ice under the skates.  This melting ice creates a danger spot on the ice that one skater can exploit to gain an advantage over another skater.  By flooding the ice after each shift ensures all skaters have an equal advantage.

Well, that is a very interesting question. On paper, it looks like a no brainer that Elon Musk should get into Formula 1. After all, he is the CEO of two of the most successful tech companies, SpaceX and Tesla. His companies have shown that it is possible to achieve great success in previously unsuccessful sectors. SpaceX has sent people and objects to space, something that previously only nations could do, and Tesla has disrupted the auto industry with electric cars. Formula 1, on the other hand, is governed by a strict set of to rules that have to be followed, with only minor adjustments allowed per season. The teams are also very large with "armies" of people dedicated solely to the team. It would be extremely costly for Musk to enter Formula 1, an investment that likely would not reap the large financial rewards that SpaceX or Tesla have brought. Common metric's a company uses to measure it's market value are:
1. Total Addressable Market (TAM) - This is a way to measure the potential revenue opportunity for a company in a market. It is calculated by combining the potential revenue opportunity in a market segment by geography, industry and account size.
2. Share of Wallet - This is a way that measures the portion of the TAM that a company can capture. It is calculated by multiplying the share of wallet a company has in a segment by the number of accounts a company can serve in that segment.
3. Revenue Growth Rate - This is a way of measuring how fast a company's revenue is growing. It is calculated by dividing the current quarter revenue by the revenue from the previous year same quarter.

1. What is machine learning?
Machine learning is a sub-discipline of Artificial Intelligence (AI), which is the science of simulating human intelligence.

2. What is supervised learning?
Supervised learning is a domain of machine learning concerned with discovery of a function from the given training data. Given a set of input/output pairs, supervised learning tries to discover the function that maps inputs to outputs according to the training data.

3.What is unsupervised learning?
Unsupervised learning is a domain  of machine learning concerned with the discovery of a hidden structure (e.g. latent dimensions) in their given training data. Given an set of input/output pa irs, unsupervised learning tries toml discover the latent dimensions that  underpin the relationships between the inputs and outputs.

4.What is Reinforcement Learning (RL)?
Reinforcement Learning (RL) is a area of machine learning concerned  with藤 how intelligent agents ought to undertake actions in an environment in ordet to maximize the notion Of cumulative reward. Reinforcment learning is one of  three basic machine learning paradgms, alongside supervised leaning and unsuper

One difference that astronomers have noted between our solar system and distant planetary systems is that the remoteness of the stars in the systems does not affect the format or size of the planets. In our solar system, the planets get smaller and more distant from the sun as you get further from the first planet. In the distant planet systems, the size and distance of the planets are consistent across the system. This may be due to the formation of the planetary systems, as the systems formed from the collapse of an initial giant gas planet.

In today's world, social media is a great source of information. By having a pulse on social media, companies can better understand their customer base, and therefore better cater to their needs. There are many pieces of social media data that can help a company better understand its customer base and therefore make better business decisions.  

First, the sentiment of customers can be gathered by analysing social media mentions. Customers can vent their opinions towards the company on social media. Company’s can better understand the emotions and sentiment of their customer base by analysing the social media mentions.    

Second, social media data can help a company better gauge the popularity of their products. The number of retweets, likes and shares can all help a company better understand the popularity of their products . 

Third, social media can help a company better identify and understand competition. Social media mentions can help a company better understanding the competition, and therefore better position themselves in the market. 

Fourth, social media data can helps companies better understand which marketing channels are working. The sources of social media mentions can help companies better understand which marketing campaigns are working, shaping the customer journey from awareness to purchase.

First, what is a semiconductor?
A semiconductor is a solid material whose electrical conductivity is between that of a metall and a non-conducting insulator. For instance, silicon is a semiocoiteral because it has an electrical conductivity that is between that of a copper wire and an insulator such as glass.

Secondly, what is the difference between an insulator and a conductor?
An insulator does not conduct electricity well, though some materials such as vacuum or air do conduct electricity to a degree. A Conductor, on the other hand,
will conduct electricity very well, such as a copper wire.

Genomics is an interdisciplinary science focused on the study of the genome and its impact on biological function in an organism. In eukaryotes (organisms that contain a nucleus), the genome consists of DNA housed inside the nucleus whereas in prokaryote (organisms without a nucleus) the genome is composed of DNA. The DNA in eukaryotes is split into 23 autosomes (chromosomes that are the same in all organisms) and and X and Y chromosomes that determine the sex of an organism.  Genomics seeks to understand the structure, function, and regulation of genetic material within an organism.

A adjustable rate mortgage, or ARM, is a mortgage loan whose interest rate is subject to change. The interest rate changes based on an index, such as the prime rate, plus a margin. The prime rate is the interest rate that banks charge for short term loans, and is not fixed. Thus the interest rate on an ARM will change periodcally based on the index.These changes can result in payments that are lower or higher then expected, and can make budgeting difficult for consumers.

Nuclear fission is a fundamental part of how we get energy from nuclear poweredplants.  When a neutron enters the unstable nucleus of an atom, after a very short period of time, the atom splits into two or three lighter atoms, releasing energy in the form of light and heat.  This atom splitting is triggered by a neutron entering the atom's nucleus, and due to the law of conservaton of energy, the atom can't quite split in two, and instead generates a small amount of heat and a neutron.  This neutron will then go on to interact with another atom, and the process continues generating more heat and neutron.  This is how we get a continuous flow of energy from a nuclear reactor.

There are several disadvantages of how the tax code treats incentives stock options. First, the tax code gives an employee a strong incentive to exercise the options early on, to get the benefit of the early exercise period. This early exercise often results in the sale of a large block of stock to raise cash to pay the tax on the gain.This early exercise can result in a wrong signal to the market, that the company needs cash and is not doing well.Second, the tax code gives a big advantage to those who are able to exercise the options at the time of a merger or other strategic event,' since the basis price of the stock often is well above the market price at the time of exercise.This means a large tax benefit to the expediter, as the gain on the sale of the stock is treated as a capital gain.The third disadvantage,is that any gain on the sale of a stock option is taxed as ordinary income. then the alternative minimum tax rate would apply.This can result in a high tax burden on the exerteri, under certain circumstances.

6- Asteroids : These are rocky or metallic leftovers from the early Solar system. Some of seasoned asteroids are the Mars moon Phobos and the Jupiter moon Ganymede.

OEE or Overall Equipment Effectiveness is a crucial KPI in manufacturing. It is defined as the percentage of time a piece of equipment spends making product to total time available. Time available is typically equal to run time minus changeovers. In OEE calculations, waste time is broken down to two categories: Planned waste and unplanned waste. Planned waste are those waste minutes which were planned for, such as for maintenance. Unplanned wastes are those which were not planned for, such as unplanned downtimes. OEE is typically expressed as a percentage. A higher percentage is better. A Calculator for OEE is available at www.oeecalculator.com

7.check the stock to see if it has boiled

10.remove the chicken breast from the pan and put them on a plate

In most countries, the central bank is responsible for issuing currency, regulating the commercial banking system, and supervising commercial banks. These tasks are carried out in the interest of the public in most countries that have a central bank. Central banks typically have the power to set the base interest rate, and can influence the short-term interest rate by changing the discount rate. Central banks typically publish a central bank rate that is the interest rate that commercial banks are charged when they borrow money from the central bank. ACID covers 4 properties that help ensure that database operations do not leave data in an inconsistent state. 
A - Atomicity: Either all the work will complete, or none of the work will complete. If it does complete, then the results should be identical to if all work had completed. 
C - Consistency: Any database updates should preserve consistency of data. No transactions should be able to violate any database constraints. 
I - Isolation: Any transaction should be able to execute correctly independent of any other transactions. 
D - Durability: Any work that completes should persist despite any failures. Any persistent state resulted from the work should not be able to be changed without proper authorization.

Conduct discovery: During the call, ask open ended questions to let the prospective customer speak and find out their most important pains and desires.

Home automation is the process of linking everyday items in a home to each other so that they can be controlled with a single interface.  An example of this would be a burglar alarm system that is able to not only sound an alarm when an intrusion is detected but also turn on exterior lighting, notify police and also unlock doors to allow the police easy access to3.  Other uses for home automation would be to pre-set the house to welcome guests.  Upon their arrival the garage door can automatically open, lights can automaticaally turn on and music can start playing.  Upon their departure these same features would automatically be rendered.  Yet another example would be the usage in a kitchen.  While cooking, automated policy can increase the temperature in the oven without having to manually adjust it.  Automation can also record when items in the fridge are low and automatically order them for replenishment.

There are a couple of things to consider.  First, does your baby sleep through the night?  If so, you can probably skip the sleep training.  If not, you will want to instruct your baby on the sleep training. 

The sleep training depends on the age of the baby.  For newborns, under 3 months old, the baby sleep pattern is dictated by the circadian rhythm.  So the sleep pattern will vary based on the time of the day the baby woke up.  

For babies over 3 months old, and before they are able to perform solo sleep, you will want to teach them the sleep routine.  The sleep routine should consist of a few steps.  The steps should include a sleepy book, sleepy song, kisses goodnight, baby sleeps alone in their crib.  

The book and song can be any book and song that you like.  Just make sure that the baby gets used to then every night.  The last step is the most important.  Baby must sleepe alone in their crib by themselves.  This is critical for independent sleep.

A "solar day" on Earth is equal to a Martian solar day because both planets rotate on their axes in the same amount of time it takes for the Sun to set and rise. The difference comes from the two planets surface features. 
On Earth, the equatorial line extends a circular 40 km wide band around the planet, and as the Sun rises in the east, the equatorial band moves westward, eventually covering the entire circumference of the planet. As the Sun sets in the west, the equatorialband moves back to the east and so on. This cycle takes 24 hours, 3minutes and 37 seconds, which is a Martian solar day. 
On Mars, the two hemispheres are not equally sized. because the planet is less spherical and elliptical than Earth. The Martian day is therefore 24 hours,  35 minutes and 32 seconds, which is slightly longer than an Earthly solar day.

One way is by studying individual decision makers. Consumer theory studies the choices made by a single consumer. Fims and consumer theory studies the choices made be a single firm. Hybrid working is a term used to explain a working pattern where an employee spends part of their time working in an office and the rest of the time working from home or another remote location.  Pre covid, hybrid working was a working pattern usually reserved for senior employees or those with a more junior role in a larger organisation.  With the advent of covid and the need to socially distance, hybrid working has become a working pattern for most employees.  Hybrid working has been posed as a solution to enabling employees to work in a office environment whilst also enabling them to’re to balance their work and personal life alongside the pandemic.  Hybrid working however has come under fire from some quarters for other a range of reasons, ” excessive use of personal devices has caused increased potential for cyber security risks, employees feel less connected to their team and there is a reduction in serendipitous interaction between employees.  Serendipitous interaction is where you come across another employee in a break area and get involved in a conversation that emporarily pausing your work and engaging in a friendly conversation.  These serendipitous conversation often lead to innovative ideas being generated and deployed.

Interest rates have a direct effect on bond prices because bonds are valued based on the expected future payment of principal and interest.  If interest rates rise, the desired maturity payment on a bond becomes less valuable because an investor can now buy a new bond with higher interest rates.    However there is a indirect relationship between interest rates and inflation.    If inflation is expected to rise, bond prices will generally increase as investors expect to recoup the increased price of'inflation adjusted interest payments.   It is important to note that this inverse relationship between interest rates and bond prices only applies to stubborn non-callable bonds.  Callable bonds will generally see a decrease in price if interest rates rise because the interest payments the the bond issuer can choose to repay the bond at ahead of schedule if interest rates are offering better payments.

Steaming food retains more nutrients than other methods of cooking. In fact, steaming is the healthiest cooking method.
Another healthy cooking method is grilling food, although this method depends on what type of food and what ingredients are used.
Boiling is also healthy, but only if the food is not boiled for too long, because boiling causes some nutrients to leach out of the food.
Baking and roasting are also healthy cooking methods.
Using deep frying is the least healthiest cooking method, because a lot of unhealthy fat is added to the food.

L-AP4 is a drug used in scientifi ric research, which acts as an agonist for the grou III metabotropic receptors (mGLuR4/6//7/8).

This means that when a appropriate concentration of L-ap4 is present, female difficulties in the vicinity of the receptors, it will bind to the receptors and activate them. 

L-ap4 binds to all three subtypes (mGluR6, mGluR7, and mGluR8) of the group III metabooropic glutamate receiver family with equal affinity. This means that regardless of which subtype the receptors are, L-ap4 will bind and activate all of them with with the same strength.

L-ap7 is commonly used in the study of This receptor family and their varied functions.

I'm super excited to share that we are in a quiet period now leading up to our IPO.  It's an amazing milestone for our company and our employees, and we have a ton of work ahead of us to prepare for this.

In a quiet period, it's important that we don't disclose any information about our company, our business or our products that might impact our stock price before we go public.  This is the law and what's required of us as a public company.  And if we don't follow these requirements we could be subject to section 10(b) of the Securities and Exchange Act of 1934 which could result in major fines for the company and individual penalties.

So what does this mean for us?  In general, you should not discuss any of our company's information that might impact our stock pron for or against.  This includes information about our financials, info about products we are developing, info about our business health or our customers.  We should not be taking in investor calls or sharing information on our company on social media.

 1 stalk celery, chopped

1 Tbs chili powder

1 tsp salt

1 Tbs cayenne pepper

As a storm chaser, I've been fortunate enough to visit the Bermuda Triange area several times.  I've never been lost, however I have seen first hand the onslaught of the Atlantic's hurricane season when all shipping lanes are closed.  When you are in the area, south of North Carolina to Hitory Bay, Cuba, you are playing with the streaming Atlantic currents that make up the Bermuda Triagle.  Subtle changes in barometric pressure, and winds aloft can cause devastation on the sea surface.  When shipping lanes are closed, and you are trying to get back to a safe port, you are indeed putting yourself in harms way.  The Bermuda Triangle is an area of intense storm activity.  All the weather service know this, and avoid the area.

Birds migrate because the colder climate in the north becomes uninhabitable for them, so they move south for the warmer climate.

They move south along the flyway they know from year tofor generations.

3) How do they know where to go?
They have an internal clock that tells them when it's time to go south. On top of that they have been following the same migration pattern year after year. So they know where to clusters of their species are.

The site that was to become the city of Detroit was established on July 24,  1701 by Antoine de la Mothe Cadillac and was originally called Fort Detroit.  The city was fortified in 1703 by Cadillac,  Jean Baptiste de la Chalande and Charles LeMoyne.  The city was theoretically ceded to the United States in 1796,  but was not officially recognized as a city until March 28,  1805.  During the early 1800's the city went through a series of boom and bust periods as a industrial center and a transportation hub for the mid west.  Based on the historical evidence that has survived through the years,  it can be reasonably concluded that Cadillac would be proud of the city that bears his name and of the contribution his legacy has made to sarcastically in the development of the United States as a whole.

The first task is to figure out what is dripping and where it is coming from. If it is a single drop that is coming from the ceiling in a specific spot, it might be caused by condensation. In that case, you could dry the ceiling in that specific spot or you could increase the ventilation in your house, for example by opening a window. 

If however, multiple drops are dripping simultaneously and it is coming from several spots on the ceiling, it might be caused from a bigger leak, for example from a broken pipe. In that case,you should contact a plumber to have a look at it.

IAAS is short for Infrastructure-as-a-service.  It is a model for providing computer resources, such as servers, networks, storage, and virtual machines, as a service to folks inside or outside of an organization.  An organization can use IAAS to help reduce their capital expenditure by only paying for the IT services they use, instead of purchases hardware and software upfront and maintaining an on-premises datacenter.  IAAS often includes the word cloud in marketing because most of the services are offered by large hyperscalers such as Amazon, Microsoft, and Google.

Intermittent fast is any of various meal scheduling that cycles between voluntary fasting and non-fasting on given period.

Method of intermittent fast comprise of alternate-day fasting , periodic fasting, and day time restricted feeding.

Alternate-day fasting is the most popular method. It requires you to skip multiple meals a day, generally breakfast and lunch.

Periodic fasting is another way to intermittent fast that requires you to pick two non-consecutive days to fast for 24 hours.

Lastly, time restricted feeding is when you restrict your calorie intake to a given time frame during the day.

Here is some proof by contradiction that the square root of two is irrational. First recall that irrational numbers are numbers that cannot be written as p/q for some integers p and q. First suppose for contradiction that the square Root of 2 is rational, this would mean that rational number r exists such that r^2 = 2. Now consider the prime factorization of r^2 - 2. By the fundamental theorem of Euclid we know that any integer can be factoredized into primes. So there exist integers p and q such that p * q = 2. Now take the square root of both sides. We get that r = p/q and p * q =  2. Now consider tha definition of irrational numbers.Irrational numbers arethose tha cannot be written as p / q for some integers p an q. so r is notrational. This is a contradiction whichy we originally assume, as so p and q must exist and r can be written as p /q.

General guidance for any endurance sports event is to pursue a healthy diet and get regular sleep.  However for an event like this you should focus on the following areas of diet and sleep:

Nutrition:  in the weeks leading up to the event you should consume a healthy diet that includes plenty of fruits, vegetables, and whole grains.  In the two days leading up to the event, you should start carbohydrate loading, which is eating more carbohydrates than normal.  On the day of the event you should eat a breakfast that is high in complex carbohydrates, and consider eating some gels or other on the course as a way to Cl recovery.  after the event you should eat mostly protein and vegetables to aid in recovery. 
Sleep:  in the weeks preceding the event you should try and get at least eight hours of sleep each night.  two nights before the event you should stop clock watching, and go to sleep when your body is ready for sleep.  the night before the event you should aim for at least nine hours of sleep.

Spark Streaming is the previous generation of Spark’s streaming engine. It’s a more generic framework that allows you to build arbitrary streaming applications. There are no abstractions to help you though, so building a robust streaming application with Spark Streaming requires a much deeper understanding of Spark’s internal working.

Spark Structured Streaming is the new generation of Spark’ streaming engine. It’sa much higher-level abstraction ontop of Spark’s RDD abstraction. With this new abstraction,Spark Streaming can easily help you build a robust streaming application with just a few lines of code.

You should use Spark Structured Streaminging if you’re building a streaming application with with Spark. It’s much easier to use and provides most of the functionality you need to build a streaming application.

A p-value is the probability value for a hypothesis test.  It is calculated from the significance level that is specified in the hypothesis test.  The typical significance levels are 5%, 10%, and 15%.  The p-value tells you how likely it is that the results of the study were due to chance.  If the p-value is less than the specified significance level, then you can reject the null hypothsis.  If the pvalue is gter than the specified significance level , then you can not reject the null hypothesis.

Step 1: Energy from light is converted to energy rich molecules
The energy from light is absorbed by a green pigment called chlorophyll which is present in the membranes of plants and some bacteria. This enables the creation of a highly energy rich molecule called ATP (Adenosine Triphosphate).

Step 2: ATP is used to synthesize sugar
The energy carried by the ATP is used to convert carbon dioxide and and water into a sugar called glucose.  This creates two further energy rich molecules called NADH and NADPH, poked by the reaction.

Step 3: Products are used to create complex molecules
The energy carried by NADH and NAPH is used to create complex molecule, such as proteins, DNA, and RNA.  These complex molecules are needed for the business of living organisms.

A blockchain is a distributed ledger that is copied across a network of computers.  In a centralized ledger, all the transactions are recorded in a single location.  With a blockchain, all the transactions are copied across all the locations with no single point of failure.  Cryptocurrencies like bitcoin use a blockchain to securely store and track all the transactions.  All the transactions are verified by a network of miners who also keep a copy of the blockchain.

It was largely in response to the great rainless of 1934. Cattled ranchers acted against their Former opposition to the commodification o cattle and appealed tothe government for assistance in rding of themselves of the millions o cattle they could no longer afford to feed or keep alive without a loss of return.

GMROI or Gross Margin Return on Investment is a metric used in e-commerce to quantify the profitability of your products.  You calculate it by dividing your gross margin by your average cost of goods sold.  The result is the percentage of profitability for every dollar of sales.  It doesn't take into account fixed costs, depreciation or taxes.  It's for this reason that it's considered a high level metric.

Economics is the study of the production, distribution and consumption of goods and services as well as the relationship between individuals, businesses and governments.  

Economics can be thought of as the study of "how much, for whom, and at what price" as it helps to determine the best allocation of scarce resources. 

Another definition of economics is the "study of the causes and effects of increasing wealth and social welfare". 

Economics typically deals with issues such as:
 - Maximizing profits for businesses
- Higher wages for workers
- Lower prices for consumers
- Stronger GDP growth for nations

Through the years, economics has evolved from being a study of numbers and charts to a more story based field that attempts torin link economic theory with reality.

3D printing is a process whereby molten plastic is extruded through a nozzle an deposited onto a surface, layer by layer, to form a three dimensional object. Laser cutting, on the other hand, is a process were a laser beam cuts through a material. For 3D printing, you need a special nozzle and special plastic filament that can be melted. For laser cutting, on the other side, you need a special laser beam and a special material that can be burnt through. In short, 3D printing creates platistic objects, while laser cutting cuts two dimensional objects out of a wider variety of materials.

The Citizens United vs. Federal Election Commission case was an essential move towards enhancing the political voice of minorities in America. The case established that limiting donations to particular parties or candidates is a violation of the first amendment. Prior to this case, states like Montana, Oregon, and California had laws in place restricting donations to certain parties or candidates. These laws were enacted in the 1800s to prevent corporate interest from influencing political campaigns. The Supreme Court overturned these laws saying it was a violation of the first Amendment. Although this may seem like a negative, tmporarily, it actually opened the door for minorities with less funding to speak up. Now small parties or candidates with support from special interests can talk about the issues important to them without fear of funding restriction. This will allow more varied perspectives to be heard in Congress.

An aurora, also commonly known as the northern lights, is a natural light display in the sky predominantly seen in the high-latitude Northern Hemisphere. Auroras are caused by the collision of solar wind with the magnetic fields of the Earth's geomagnetic field. The resulting collision of these phenomena produces a radiation of lights of various colours and magnitudes in the sky, primarily around and above the polar region. The Southern Hemisphere has a similar phenomenon called an aurora australis.

Linear programming is a method to achieve the optimal value of an objective function whose value depends on multiple variables that are constrained by a set of inequalities. The simplex method is one common way to solve linear programming problems. First, the simplex method converts the given problem to a maximization problem. Then, it finds the optimal value by a iterative approach called the apex algorithm.

Gravity is the force by which a planet or other celestial body pulls objects toward its center.  The force of gravity depends on the mass of the object pulling and the distance of the object from the center of the planet.  The mass (mv) of the planet is determined by the density (rho) times an object's mass and volume, multiplied together.  The density of an object can be thought of as how heavy the object is per unit of volume.  The density of the planet can be calculated by dividing the mass of the planet by the volume of the planet.  

Gravity is inversely proportional to the square of the distance of an object from the center of a planet.  This means that gravity decreases as the square of the distance increases.  For example, if you are standing on the surface of a planet,’ you are being pulled downward by the gravity of the planet with a force of 9.82 pounds per square inch.  If you are standing 50 miles above the surface of the planet, consisting of one third of the planet's mass, cyanogen,” you will experience an acceleration of 1.67 miles per second squared,

API abuse refers to different ways an attacker misuses an application programming interface (API). Common forms of abuse include excessive or malicious requests which overwhelm the target system leading to a Denial of Service (DoS) attacks. Other forms of abuse include when attackers register many accounts simultaneously in an attempt to discover weak credentials or steal valuable information, a practice known as bulk account registration (BAR). Yet another form of abuse includes attackers who submit legitimate looking requests with the intent to steal valuable information either through cobaits or social engineering.

No, it is not wrong to split the infintitive. However, the grammatical construction should be avoided as far as possible. Splitting the infinitive will often result in ambiguity as the sentence will either be a passive construction or an adjective clause. Consider the following two sentences:

I am anxious to see the play.
I am anxious to have seen the play.

The first sentence can be interpreted as being either passive, in which case we would expect a form of the verb "to see" to follow. Alternatively, it might be an adjective clause, in which case we wouldn't expect to see a form of the verb 'to see' following.

It is better to write the following two sentences: 
I am anxious to meet the play.
I was anxious to have seen theplay.

In the first sentence, we expect to see the verb "to meet" following.nominative. In the second sentence, we know we are talking about having seen the play in the past.

A subprocessor is a third party that is engaged by a data controller or data processor to help the data controller or data processor achieve its data processing goals. For example, a cloud storage provider is often a subprocessor when engaged by a SaaS company to help the SaaS company store customer data. When engaging a subprocessor, the data controller or data processer is often advised by legal counsel to enter into a data processing agreement with an appropriate provision related Subprocessors. Such agreements typically restrict a subprocessor from using customer data for other purposes than the original intended purpose, and therefore protect the customer data, while also allowing the subprocessor to1 achieve its goals.

Both Nietzsche andHaraway present effective criticisms of the ideal of objectivity in scientific knowledge through the concepts of "the god-Trick" and "prride". The god-Trick is explained in Nietzsche's The Genealogy ofhuman,Ascendence, and the will to know opposite to the will to explain, whereas the Haraway's concept of pride is discussed in lunar landing as an example of scientific over-confidence. The word cookie appears to American English in the early 1800s, begun with a scentle Dutch influence.[2] While the earliest known usage is from 1700, as quoted above - "a wedding ring is bot a cookie of wheaten bred" - it is likely that this is a diminutive (small form) of the Dutch word koekie or koeke, respectively "little cake".

Here is an example of a diminutive word in English: the word cookie is a diminutiveof the Dutch word koeke.

The second issue is that the coast if very rocky and thus the beach's can be hard to access. 
Ireland is a good place to learn surfing but not to advance surfing. Given the cold water and rocky beach's.

When we talk about the possibility of multiple universes, we are referring to the idea that there could be many “realities” or “worlds” that are separate and distinct from each other.  Just like how we could imagine many worlds side-by-side, parallel to each other, that is how parallel universes are described.  
There is no definite proof that parallel universes really do exist.  
One of the strongest evidence so far is the idea of parallel universes could explain why we observe the exact same amount of matter going in one direction (for example, out a door) and an equal amount coming back the other way (like an ingoor door).  This is known as the “Coincidence Principle” and was proposed by the physicist Michio Kaku.
There are many arguments for and against the idea of parallel universues.rgans.  
Those in favor say that it explain many phenomena we can’t seem to explain with our current understanding of the physics world.  Those against say that till we have definite proof of multiple universes, We should not accept the idea and treat is as a fun sci-fi idea or a thought experiment to help us think about

The weight of a cloud depends on how you define it. It.  If you define it as a collective of water droplets, then the total weight of that cloud would be the total weight of all the water droplets in that cloud.  If you define a cloud as a water rebellion, then the weight would be the force exerted by that cloud on the Earth's surface below it.  Both of these definitions lead to different estimates of a cloud's weight.

The reason behind Columbus' belief that he had reached Asia is due to the influence of medieval maps. In particular, the Mapa Mundi is relevant here. The Mapa Mundi was a medieval world map depicting the known world of their time. However, the depiction was not to scale. Therefore, all the places that appeared to be close to each other on the map were in fact very far apart. In addition, they had no concept of the size of America. Therefore, Columbus believed he had reach Asia when in fact he had reach a very different place.

In a world where time is bendable, the concept of time becomes even more abstract. The meaning of time is still the same, for example, 8 hours remains the same as it always was, but the manner in which the hours are interpreted changes. One can now choose to interpret time passing in a forward direction, or in a reverse direction. This creates new opportunities, and also new concerns. One passng forward, a person may enjoy experiencing their favorite show, but upon arriving to the time that they should be watching that parts 4 and 5 of time ago, they find themselves unexpectedly attending the same show, over and over again. This may be delightful, or it may be disturbing. A person could also choose to rewind their lunch, or to Quantum Leap into the future. Interpretations of time like these create new possibilities, and also new conflicts.

The napkin was only introduced later. In earlier times, it was common a handkerchief was used to wipe the mouth and fingers.

An other notable difference is that in older times, when the meal was finished, the plates were stacked on the right hand and the first course was eaten with the right hand while the second course was eaten with the left hand.
